memoryArtifacts.headline: Sarah Fowler and husband Sylvanus Covey (1818 - 1911) Find a Grave by Lorna Puleo
memoryArtifacts.description:
Birth: 	Aug. 22, 1822
Death: 	Nov. 21, 1888

 
Family links: 
 Spouse:
  Sylvanus Covey (1818 - 1911)
 
 Children:
  Cyrenus P. Covey (1849 - 1873)*
  Irene Covey Southard (1851 - 1936)*
  Lorraine Covey Turner (1856 - 1934)*
  Sylvanus Hartwell Covey (1858 - 1939)*
 
*Calculated relationship
 
Burial:
Maple Grove Cemetery 
Worcester
Otsego County
New York, USA

Record added: Jun 21, 2010

zh.lifeSummary: Sarah Fowler於1822年8月22日出生在美國紐約州格林。她於1842年與Sylvanis S Covey Sr在美國紐約州格林結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她於1870年住在美國紐約州奧瑟格庫斯東且在美國紐約州奧瑟格德卡度住了大約5年。她去世於1888年11月21日在美國紐約州奧瑟格伍斯特享年66歲，埋葬在美國紐約州奧瑟格伍斯特馬普爾 葛洛夫 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sarah Fowler于1822年8月22日出生在美国纽约州格林。她于1842年与Sylvanis S Covey Sr在美国纽约州格林结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她于1870年住在美国纽约州奧瑟格庫斯東且在美国纽约州奧瑟格德卡度住了大约5年。她去世于1888年11月21日在美国纽约州奧瑟格伍斯特享年66岁，埋葬在美国纽约州奧瑟格伍斯特馬普爾 葛洛夫 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Fowler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a fowler, a hunter or trapper of wild birds (a common medieval occupation), from Middle English fogheler, fugheler (Old English fugelere, a derivative of fugol ‘bird’).

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: The Crimes Act
historicEvents.description: The Crimes Act was made to provide a clearer punishment of certain crimes against the United States. Part of it includes: Changing the maximum sentence of imprisonment to be increased from seven to ten years and changing the maximum fine from $5,000 to $10,000.

historicEvents.title: Slavery Becomes Illegal in New York State
historicEvents.description: During the years 1799 to 1827, New York went through a period of gradual emancipation. A Gradual Emancipation Law was passed in 1799 which freed slave children born after July 4, 1799. However, they were indentured until 25 years old for women and 28 years old for men. A law passed 1817 which freed slaves born before 1799, yet delayed their emancipation for ten years. All remaining slaves were freed in New York State on July 4, 1827.
U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.
Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

historicEvents.title: The Battle at Gettysburg
historicEvents.description: The Battle of Gettysburg involved the largest number of casualties of the entire Civil war and is often described as the war's turning point. Between 46,000 and 51,000 soldiers lost their lives during the three-day Battle. To honor the fallen soldiers, President Abraham Lincoln read his historic Gettysburg Address and helped those listening by redefining the purpose of the war.

historicEvents.title: A Treaty with Hawaii
historicEvents.description: In the Mid 1870s, The United States sought out the Kingdom of Hawaii to make a free trade agreement. The Treaty gave the Hawaiians access to the United States agricultural markets and it gave the United States a part of land which later became Pearl Harbor.

historicEvents.title: A New Civil Rights Act
historicEvents.description: During the response to civil rights violations to African Americans, the bill was passed giving African Americans equal treatment in public accommodations, public transportation, and to prohibit exclusion from jury duty. While many in the public opposed this law, the African Americans greatly favored it.

en.lifeSummary: Sarah Fowler was born on 22 August 1822, in Greene, New York, United States. She married Sylvanis S Covey Sr in 1842, in Greene, New York, United States. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 7 daughters. She lived in Cooperstown, Otsego, New York, United States in 1870 and Decatur, Otsego, New York, United States for about 5 years. She died on 21 November 1888, in Worcester, Otsego, New York, United States, at the age of 66, and was buried in Maple Grove Cemetery, Worcester, Otsego, New York, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Sarah Fowler est née le 22 août 1822, à Greene, New York, États-Unis. Elle a épousé Sylvanis S Covey Sr en 1842, à Greene, New York, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 7 filles. Elle a vécu à Cooperstown, Otsego, New York, États-Unis en 1870 et à Decatur, Otsego, New York, États-Unis pendant environ 5 ans. Elle est décédée le 21 novembre 1888, à Worcester, Otsego, New York, États-Unis, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterrée à Maple Grove Cemetery, Worcester, Otsego, New York, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Sarah Fowler wurde am 22. August 1822 in Greene, New York, Vereinigte Staaten geboren. Sie hat 1842 in Greene, New York, Vereinigte Staaten Sylvanis S Covey Sr geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 7 Töchter. 1870 hat sie in Cooperstown, Otsego, New York, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt, und ungefähr 5 Jahre lang hat sie in Decatur, Otsego, New York, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Sie starb am 21. November 1888 im Alter von 66 Jahren in Worcester, Otsego, New York, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Maple Grove Cemetery, Worcester, Otsego, New York, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Sarah Fowler è nata il il 22 agosto 1822 nel Greene, New York, Stati Uniti d'America. Lei sposato Sylvanis S Covey Sr nel 1842, nel Greene, New York, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 7 figlie. Vissuto a Cooperstown, Otsego, New York, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1870 e nel Decatur, Otsego, New York, Stati Uniti d'America per circa 5 anni. Morì il 21 novembre 1888, nel Worcester, Otsego, New York, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 66 anni, e fu sepolta a Maple Grove Cemetery, Worcester, Otsego, New York, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.Maple Grove Cemetery, Worcester, Otsego,: アメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州オトセゴウオルセスターマプル グローブ セメテリー
ja.Cooperstown, Otsego, New York, United St: アメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州オトセゴコオペルストウン
ja.lifeSummary: Sarah Fowlerは1822年8月22日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州グレエンで生まれた。1842年にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州グレエンでSylvanis S Covey Srと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1870年にはアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州オトセゴコオペルストウンに，アメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州オトセゴデカトゥルに約5年間住んでいた。彼女は1888年11月21日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州オトセゴウオルセスターで66歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州オトセゴウオルセスターマプル グローブ セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.East Worcester, Otsego, New York, United: 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고 이스트 워스스터
ko.<East Worcester, Otsego, New York>: 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고 이스트 워스스터
ko.Maple Grove Cemetery, Worcester, Otsego,: 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고 워스스터 마플 그로브 세멧리
ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Fowler(은)는 1822년 8월 22일 미국 뉴욕 주 그린에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1842년 미국 뉴욕 주 그린에서 Sylvanis S Covey Sr(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 1870년도에 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고 쿠퍼스타운에서, 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고 데카투르에서 약 5년 동안 살았다. 그녀는 1888년 11월 21일 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고 워스스터에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고 워스스터 마플 그로브 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sarah Fowler nasceu em 22 de agosto de 1822, em Greene, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América. Ela casou-se com Sylvanis S Covey Sr em 1842, em Greene, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela viveu em Cooperstown, Otsego, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América em 1870 e em Decatur, Otsego, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América por quase 5 anos. Ela faleceu em 21 de novembro de 1888, em Worcester, Otsego, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América, com 66 anos, e foi sepultada em Maple Grove Cemetery, Worcester, Otsego, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Sarah Fowler родилась 22 августа 1822 г. в местности грин, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sylvanis S Covey Sr в 1842 г. в местности грин, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 7 дочерей. Она жила в местности купирстоун, оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты в 1870 году и в местности декатур, оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты около 5 лет. Она умерла 21 ноября 1888 г. в местности ворсестир, оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 66 лет и была похоронена в местности мапл гров кеметири, ворсестир, оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Sarah Fowler nació el 22 de agosto de 1822, en Greene, Nueva York, Estados Unidos. Se casó con Sylvanis S Covey Sr en 1842, en Greene, Nueva York, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 7 hijas. Vivió en Cooperstown, Otsego, Nueva York, Estados Unidos en 1870 y en Decatur, Otsego, Nueva York, Estados Unidos durante unos 5 años. Murió el 21 de noviembre de 1888, en Worcester, Otsego, Nueva York, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 66 años, y fue sepultada en Maple Grove Cemetery, Worcester, Otsego, Nueva York, Estados Unidos.
